rows.notes: Clear centered title at top of page, fully legible handwriting and no amounts in this line.
Clear centered subtitle immediately under the title; no monetary columns present on this line.
Left-margin section label for the group of entries below. The date and section heading are legible though written in the margin.
Monetary columns well aligned with ruling lines; pounds=20, shillings=6, pence=8 clearly written and within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Stipend paid at Christmas (entered against the Dividend book).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid at Christmas from the Dividend book, amounting to £20 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount reads as 27.0.0 with clear alignment. The place/name is slightly abbreviated but the numeric columns are unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Augmentation paid to the scholar Wheaton.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation paid to the scholar Wheaton, amounting to £27 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Pounds, shillings and pence are aligned and legible; the name 'Shiers' is slightly stylized but readable. All currency values within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Stipend paid to Mr Shier.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid to Mr Shier, amounting to £33 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Large pounds value '600.0.0' very clear and well aligned with ruling lines. Description text readable enough to identify as augmentation for two chaplains.
rows.english_description: Augmentation to the common fund and payments for two chaplains.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation to the common fund and payments for two chaplains, amounting to £600 0s 0d.

rows.description: Rectori, e legato Jnis Bray, pro rata anni past.
rows.notes: Numeric columns for 5.9.2 are clear and within valid ranges. Some letters in the description (Jnis/legato) are faded/abbreviated; chosen reading based on common phrasing and surrounding letters.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Rector from the legacy of John Bray as pro rata for the past year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector from the legacy of John Bray as pro rata for the past year, amounting to £5 9s 2d.

rows.notes: Amount '25.0.0' clearly written and aligned. Description legible though Latin abbreviated; reading chosen from visible letter shapes.
rows.english_description: Payment to the same (the Rector) in lieu of an ecclesiastical benefice.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector in lieu of an ecclesiastical benefice, amounting to £25 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Left-margin section header introducing next group; handwriting clear and date visible.
Amount '34.13.4' is well aligned and legible. Parenthetical reference p231 visible. No fraction after pence.
rows.english_description: Stipend paid at the Annunciation festival.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid at the Annunciation festival, amounting to £34 13s 4d.

rows.description: Kidlington pro Augment: Comm: pro 3 quarteris
rows.notes: Clear reading of '45.7.6'. The place-name 'Kidlington' and note about 3 quarters is legible; currency columns align with ruling lines.
rows.english_description: Augmentation for Kidlington to the common fund covering three quarters of the year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation for Kidlington to the common fund for three quarters of the year, amounting to £45 7s 6d.

rows.notes: Pounds and shillings clearly '112.12'. Pence reads as '4' with a small loop/mark after it interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny = 0.5). The fractional mark is faint; alternate readings possible, hence moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Payment to L. Witt on account of the 1795 annual debt.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to L. Witt on account of the 1795 annual debt, amounting to £112 12s 4½d.

rows.description: L. Witt Deb pro semestri ad triennis (p234)
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings read as '103.1'. Pence shows '1' with a tiny superscript/loop that looks like a farthing mark ('q' = 0.25). The fraction is faint and ambiguous; chosen 0.25 based on the small tail resembling a farthing sign.
rows.english_description: Payment to L. Witt for the semester under a three-year arrangement.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to L. Witt for the semester under a three-year arrangement, amounting to £103 1s 1¼d.

rows.notes: Amount '102.3.8' clearly aligned and legible; parenthetical references 235/236 visible. Description readable.
rows.english_description: Payment to Merton College for the two above-mentioned semesters.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to Merton College for the two above-mentioned semesters, amounting to £102 3s 8d.

rows.description: Rectori ad Annunc. ut supra ad Nativ:
rows.notes: Numeric '30.9.2' clear. Description is abbreviated Latin but matches phrasing used elsewhere on page; alignment with column lines confirms amounts.
rows.english_description: Payment to the Rector at the Annunciation as above (see Nativity entry).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector at the Annunciation as above, amounting to £30 9s 2d.

rows.notes: Left-margin label for the next festival (St. John Baptist). Abbreviations (D: J: Bapt:) are slightly compressed but interpretable in context.
Amount '20.6.8' clear and aligned. Parenthetical page reference p240 visible. Description abbreviations consistent with earlier entries.
rows.english_description: Stipend paid at the feast of St John the Baptist (Midsummer).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid at the feast of St John the Baptist, amounting to £20 6s 8d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid to Mr Shier for the stated festival, amounting to £33 6s 8d.

rows.notes: Clear numeric '50.0.0' aligned to pounds column. Description (augmentation for governor/moderator and subdeacon) is legible though Latin abbreviations present.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation to the common fund together with payments to the moderator and subdeacon, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.description: Rectori ad fest D:J:B ut supra ad Annun.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector at the feast of St John the Baptist as above for the Annunciation, amounting to £30 9s 2d.

Amount '27.0.0' clearly written. Place name reads as 'Gwinear' (common parish name) – letters are legible enough for that reading but slightly stylized.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Stipend paid for Gwinear at Michaelmas 1796, amounting to £27 0s 0d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Augmentation to the common fund for Long Witt, amounting to £34 15s 6d.

rows.description: Rectori ad Micl ut supra ad D:J:Bapt.
rows.notes: Amount '30.9.2' legible. Description mirrors earlier 'Rectori' lines and is read by analogy; some abbreviations present but consistent.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment to the Rector at Michaelmas as above for the feast of St John the Baptist, amounting to £30 9s 2d.
